facts.description: District ranked highest by normalized weighted water-loss anomaly score after applying profile mapping and exclusion rules.
facts.canonical_answer: Old Mill is the top anomaly district with score 95.7
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Old Mill is shown as the highest anomaly-score district with score near 95.7.

facts.description: District with the largest Q4 versus Q3 water-loss-rate change in percentage points.
facts.canonical_answer: Harbor Flats has the fastest loss-rate increase at about 5.8 percentage points
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Harbor Flats is identified as the largest loss-rate increase with about 5.8 percentage points.

facts.description: District with the highest Q4 water-loss volume in kiloliters.
facts.canonical_answer: Southworks Main has the highest Q4 water-loss volume at 4,940 kL
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Southworks Main is shown as the loss-volume hotspot near 4,940 kL.

facts.description: District with the highest Q4 pressure-alert rate per 1,000 service connections.
facts.canonical_answer: Airport Spur has the highest pressure-alert rate at about 97.6 per 1,000 connections
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Airport Spur is identified as the pressure-alert rate hotspot.

facts.description: Region with the highest aggregate Q4 water-loss volume.
facts.canonical_answer: South is the region with the highest Q4 water-loss volume at 9,110 kL
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if South is shown as the regional loss hotspot near 9,110 kL.

facts.description: Count of districts at or above the high-priority anomaly-score threshold.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if the visual states that 2 districts are high priority under the configured threshold.

facts.description: Median Q4 district water-loss rate for context.
facts.canonical_answer: Median Q4 loss rate is about 7.8%
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if a median or network-wide context loss-rate value close to the expected value is included.

facts.description: Do not use forecast, subtotal, test, or old-period rows as final water-loss metrics.
facts.canonical_answer: Invalid water network rows must not be used as final metrics.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Reject or cap if invalid rows are treated as final metrics. Exclusion notes are allowed.

calculation_notes: Join district readings to profiles by zone_id. Use only actual closed records. Q3 baseline months are 2026-07 through 2026-09; Q4 analysis months are 2026-10 through 2026-12. Water-loss volume is pumped minus billed usage. Loss rate is loss divided by pumped water. Anomaly score min-max normalizes Q4 loss rate, loss-rate change, alert rate per 1,000 connections, and criticality, then applies weights 0.35, 0.25, 0.20, and 0.20. The high threshold is 70.0, with tie-breaks by rounded score, Q4 loss volume, district criticality, and district name. Median Q4 loss rate is used as context. Top anomaly district is Old Mill at 95.7. Fastest loss-rate increase is Harbor Flats at 5.8 percentage points. Loss-volume hotspot is Southworks Main at 4,940 kL. Pressure-alert hotspot is Airport Spur at 97.6 per 1,000 connections. Regional loss hotspot is South at 9,110 kL. High-priority district count is 2 and median Q4 loss rate is 7.8%.
